formatter.refined_input: Silicon, Oxygen; Functions: essential components for glass production, providing structural framework and transparency.
formatter.verification_rules: The translation accurately identifies the primary components of glass (Silicon and Oxygen) and describes their functions in glass production, aligning with the desired outcome of making glass. However, it slightly deviates from a direct translation of 'I want to make glass' by providing additional information on the components' roles, which is still relevant and useful in the context of a Yeast Products Database, considering the biochemical aspects of silicon and oxygen in biological systems.

search_results.name: Glycerol
search_results.description:
Product: Glycerol
Description: Main byproduct acting as redox sink for NAD+ regeneration during 2,3-butanediol fermentation, reaching 85.1 g/L in fed-batch cultivation
gRNA: N/A
Function: The main byproduct acts as a redox sink for NAD+ regeneration during 2,3-butanediol fermentation and reaches 85.1 g/L in fed-batch cultivation.
PubChem Data:
IUPAC: glycerol
PubChem CID: 753
PubChem Description: Glycerol is a triol with a structure of propane substituted at positions 1, 2 and 3 by hydroxy groups. It has a role as a geroprotector, an osmolyte, a detergent, a solvent, a mouse metabolite, a Saccharomyces cerevisiae metabolite, an Escherichia coli metabolite, a human metabolite and an algal metabolite. It is an alditol and a triol.
